# DI Registration Checklist: Phase 6 - DI Registration Cleanup

**Purpose**: Validate that the Phase 6 requirements clearly define dependency-registration ownership, behavior preservation, and scope boundaries before implementation planning.
**Created**: 2026-06-04
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

**Review Outcome (2026-06-04)**: All 38 items were reviewed against `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, and the constitution. All items are requirement-quality gates and are satisfied after documentation remediation. No items are N/A. No items are deferred to runtime implementation verification.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are application-owned registration requirements defined even when no current application-only services require registration? [Completeness, Spec FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Are infrastructure-owned registration requirements defined for repositories, persistence, identity, time, rule helpers, and infrastructure-backed service implementations? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, Spec User Story 3]
- [x] CHK003 Are host-owned request-handling concerns explicitly separated from application and infrastructure dependency ownership? [Completeness, Spec FR-004]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for preserving Phase 5 business rules after the registration cleanup? [Completeness, Spec FR-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for preserving credential validation and session validation behavior? [Completeness, Spec FR-010]
- [x] CHK006 Are requirements defined for preserving deterministic time and working-day helper availability? [Completeness, Spec FR-011]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is "one clear registration entry point" specific enough to guide planning without allowing multiple competing entry points per owning layer? [Clarity, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-002]
- [x] CHK008 Is "startup composition" used consistently to mean the host-level composition surface? [Clarity, Spec FR-003, Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK009 Is the clarified ownership decision unambiguous: application exposes a registration entry point while infrastructure registers infrastructure-backed implementations? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications]
- [x] CHK010 Are behavior-preservation requirements specific about routes, bodies, pagination, sessions, claims, statuses, and structured error payloads? [Clarity, Spec FR-005]
- [x] CHK011 Is the local database dependency phrased clearly enough to distinguish Phase 5 migration readiness from Phase 6 schema changes? [Clarity, Spec SC-006, Spec Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do the registration ownership requirements align with the clarification that infrastructure-backed implementations remain registered by infrastructure? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications, Spec FR-002]
- [x] CHK013 Do the no-schema-change requirements align with the assumption that the Phase 5 migration should already be applied before manual validation? [Consistency, Spec FR-007, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK014 Do the behavior-preservation requirements align with the scope boundary that Phase 6 is wiring-only? [Consistency, Spec FR-005, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK015 Are the edge cases consistent with the functional requirements and free of hidden Phase 7 feature expansion? [Consistency, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-007]
- [x] CHK016 Do success criteria avoid contradicting the no-database-change requirement while still requiring local database startup validation? [Consistency, Spec SC-005, Spec SC-006]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Can startup readability be objectively reviewed using the acceptance scenarios and success criteria? [Measurability, Spec User Story 1, Spec SC-003]
- [x] CHK018 Can behavior preservation be objectively verified from the listed response, session, and error-payload stability requirements? [Measurability, Spec User Story 2, Spec FR-005]
- [x] CHK019 Is the "under 2 minutes" reviewability outcome tied to clear registration-owner evidence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-003]
- [x] CHK020 Is the "zero direct application or infrastructure service registrations" outcome scoped to the startup surface with no ambiguity? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK021 Is "100% of representative manual smoke checks" bounded by named workflow categories? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-002]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are primary maintainer-review, runtime-preservation, and reviewer-audit flows all covered by independent user stories? [Coverage, Spec User Scenarios]
- [x] CHK023 Are future dependency additions covered as a scenario so ownership remains clear beyond the first implementation? [Coverage, Spec User Story 1]
- [x] CHK024 Are authenticated and expected-failure flows covered so compatibility is not limited to happy paths? [Coverage, Spec User Story 2]
- [x] CHK025 Are static-review flows covered for application, infrastructure, and host-owned concerns? [Coverage, Spec User Story 3]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK026 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not create, remove, or modify database schema objects? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-007]
- [x] CHK027 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not move business rules into startup composition or controllers? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK028 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not introduce Phase 7 authorization or domain features? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK029 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not reintroduce direct data-access dependencies into service classes? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK030 Are requirements explicit about compatibility with the local database after the Phase 5 migration has been applied? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, Spec Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Is Phase 5 completion documented as a dependency for Phase 6 readiness? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK032 Is Phase 5 migration application documented as a prerequisite for manual runtime validation? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK033 Is the distinction between specification/planning work and database mutation clearly documented? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK034 Are existing session authentication and compatibility error-code assumptions documented so Phase 6 cannot normalize them accidentally? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK035 Are there any remaining terms such as "owning layer", "startup surface", or "entry point" that need definition before task generation? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-003]
- [x] CHK036 Does the spec avoid conflict between application-owned contracts and infrastructure-owned implementation registrations? [Conflict, Spec Clarifications]
- [x] CHK037 Does the spec avoid implying that local test data setup is part of the Phase 6 feature itself? [Conflict, Spec FR-007, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK038 Are static checks required by the spec clearly tied to requirement quality rather than unspecified implementation preferences? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-012]

## Notes

- This checklist validates requirement quality for Phase 6. It does not verify implementation behavior.
- Implementation verification remains covered by `tasks.md`, automated tests, static scans, and local smoke validation.
